title: Combinatorics, Conditional Probability, and Simulation · Decision Defense
summary: Analyze staffing reliability, screening results, and queue risks. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a simulated operational risk model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose multiplication, permutations, combinations, or inclusion-exclusion from event structure.
Update probabilities with conditional information and Bayes reasoning.
Test independence and distinguish it from mutually exclusive events.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: An outcome pays $40 with probability 0.40 and $0 otherwise. Calculate its expected value.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Weight each outcome by its probability: 40 × 0.40 + 0 × 0.60.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Weight each outcome by its probability: 40 × 0.40 + 0 × 0.60.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.01 of the exact value and includes $ or dollars.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Monte Carlo applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Design reproducible simulations and quantify Monte Carlo sampling error.

hints: Name the Monte Carlo precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.
